threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still don’t know that the number 48 bus is the real lifeline, not the tube. Just wait till you're stuck in a tube strike.
The 133 at 2 AM is like a moving soap opera—last week, two guys argued over a half-eaten kebab while a woman danced to her headphones.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a kid selling homemade lemonade outside the kebab shop, while the bus drivers were on strike. That's Neukoelln for you.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain can you find a club that doubles as a thrift store, where DJs spin while you sift through vintage denim. It's a real vibe.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell when someone is from here if they know the quickest way to bypass the BART delays on the weekends, or if they’ve perfected the art of dodging those cable car tourists.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Last week, I saw a kid in a superhero cape hand a Muni driver a flower. Driver just smiled, waved it off, and kept driving. Classic SF moment.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think they can grab a quick bite at 2 PM, but most locals won’t even start lunch until after 3. It’s a whole different rhythm here.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by the way they order a 'café con leche' without knowing it’s served in a bowl here. Nothing screams tourist more than that.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think 'the tube' means London's whole network, not just the Overground and Shanks' Pony for local trips.
Last week on the 59, a guy brought a whole roast chicken and offered bites to anyone who looked hungry. Brixton spirit at its finest!

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I found a kebab shop serving vegan döner at 3 AM, I knew this was home. Only in Neukölln do cravings meet creativity at that hour.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You ever notice how the U-Bahn stations have some of the most random art? Like those murals celebrating ignored East German pop culture. It’s so Berlin.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a true local when they know to avoid the BART at Civic Center after 5 PM—too many loud bangs and smoke for a smooth commute.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Witnessed a guy trying to negotiate with a pigeon on the 8 bus. Pigeon was winning. Just another day in the Tenderloin.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often think 7pm is too early for dinner, but in Gràcia, that’s when the streets start buzzing with locals heading out for tapas.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by the way they fumble for the right fare on the TMB, clutching a handful of coins like it's a treasure map.
